message:
feat(label): extend --label to create/quick, add list columns, disambiguate CWD

- Add --label/-l flag to create and quick commands with same validation
  as spawn (bd-3cu02.5)
- Add PROJECT and LABEL columns to ntm list when labeled sessions exist;
  columns hidden when no labels for visual cleanliness (bd-3cu02.6)
- Add BaseProject and Label fields to SessionListItem JSON output
- Sort list by base project, then unlabeled-first, then label alphabetically
- Implement three-tier CWD disambiguation in inferSessionFromCWD:
  prefer unlabeled base session, then alphabetical first (bd-3cu02.8)
- Close beads bd-3cu02.5, bd-3cu02.6, bd-3cu02.8

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -723,10 +723,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-3cu02.2","title":"Implement ParseSessionLabel and FormatSessionName primitives","description":"## Goal
Implement ParseSessionLabel() and FormatSessionName() as the canonical label parsing primitives. These are the foundation every other change builds on.

## Context
NTM session names will support an optional goal label via `--` separator: `myproject--frontend`. The session name IS the primary key for tmux sessions, project directories, pane names, Agent Mail identity, and CASS context. By embedding the label in the session name, all existing downstream code works automatically.

## Implementation

### File: internal/config/label.go

```go
package config

import "strings"

// ParseSessionLabel splits a session name into base project name and optional label.
// The separator is the FIRST occurrence of "--" (double-dash).
// Returns (base, label) where label is empty if no "--" present.
//
// Examples:
//   ParseSessionLabel("myproject")          -> ("myproject", "")
//   ParseSessionLabel("my-project--frontend") -> ("my-project", "frontend")
//   ParseSessionLabel("foo--bar--baz")      -> ("foo", "bar--baz")
func ParseSessionLabel(sessionName string) (base, label string) {
    idx := strings.Index(sessionName, "--")
    if idx < 0 {
        return sessionName, ""
    }
    return sessionName[:idx], sessionName[idx+2:]
}

// FormatSessionName constructs a labeled session name from base and label.
// If label is empty, returns base unchanged.
func FormatSessionName(base, label string) string {
    if label == "" {
        return base
    }
    return base + "--" + label
}

// HasLabel returns true if the session name contains a label separator.
func HasLabel(sessionName string) bool {
    return strings.Contains(sessionName, "--")
}

// SessionBase returns just the base project name, stripping any label.
// Convenience wrapper around ParseSessionLabel.
func SessionBase(sessionName string) string {
    base, _ := ParseSessionLabel(sessionName)
    return base
}
```

### File: internal/config/label_test.go
Comprehensive table-driven tests covering:
- No label: "myproject" -> ("myproject", "")
- Simple label: "myproject--frontend" -> ("myproject", "frontend")
- Dashes in base: "my-project--frontend" -> ("my-project", "frontend")
- Multiple separators: "foo--bar--baz" -> ("foo", "bar--baz")
- Label with dashes: "proj--my-label" -> ("proj", "my-label")
- Empty base: "--frontend" -> ("", "frontend") -- this is invalid but the parser shouldn't crash
- Empty label: "myproject--" -> ("myproject", "") -- edge case
- FormatSessionName round-trip: FormatSessionName(ParseSessionLabel(x)) == x for all valid inputs
- HasLabel correctness
- SessionBase correctness

## Acceptance Criteria
- All functions implemented in internal/config/label.go
- All tests pass: go test -run TestParseSessionLabel ./internal/config/
- go vet and golangci-lint pass
- No imports outside stdlib","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:35.289602183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:26.534683489Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:26.534663572Z","close_reason":"implemented: label primitives, validation, spawn flag, tests, GetProjectDir","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","label-feature"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:35.289602183Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:04.683792884Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cu02.3","title":"Implement ValidateLabel function","description":"## Goal
Add a ValidateLabel() function and update ValidateSessionName() to correctly handle the `--` separator in labeled session names.

## Context
The current session name regex is `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$` in internal/tmux/session.go:29. This ALREADY allows `--` since `-` is permitted and `+` means one-or-more. So `myproject--frontend` already passes validation.

However, we need a separate ValidateLabel() function to enforce label-specific rules, and we should ensure ValidateSessionName still works correctly when the full labeled name is passed.

## Implementation

### File: internal/config/label.go (add to existing file from task 2)

```go
// ValidateLabel checks if a label string is valid.
// Rules:
// - Must start with alphanumeric character
// - Can contain alphanumeric, dash, underscore
// - Must not be empty
// - Must not exceed 50 characters
// - Must not itself contain "--" (the separator)
func ValidateLabel(label string) error {
    if label == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("label must not be empty")
    }
    if len(label) > 50 {
        return fmt.Errorf("label must not exceed 50 characters (got %d)", len(label))
    }
    if strings.Contains(label, "--") {
        return fmt.Errorf("label must not contain '--' (reserved as separator)")
    }
    if !labelRegex.MatchString(label) {
        return fmt.Errorf("label must start with alphanumeric and contain only alphanumeric, dash, underscore")
    }
    return nil
}

var labelRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$`)
```

### Verify existing ValidateSessionName
Confirm that `ValidateSessionName("myproject--frontend")` returns nil (valid). If it does, no changes needed to session.go. If not, update the regex.

Check: internal/tmux/session.go:29 -- `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$` -- this DOES match `myproject--frontend` because `--` is just two consecutive `-` characters, which are individually allowed.

### Tests
- ValidateLabel("frontend") -> nil
- ValidateLabel("my-label") -> nil
- ValidateLabel("my_label_123") -> nil
- ValidateLabel("") -> error (empty)
- ValidateLabel("a" * 51) -> error (too long)
- ValidateLabel("my--label") -> error (contains separator)
- ValidateLabel("-frontend") -> error (starts with dash)
- ValidateLabel("_frontend") -> error (starts with underscore)
- Verify ValidateSessionName("myproject--frontend") -> nil (no change needed)
- Verify ValidateSessionName("myproject--") works or fails gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- ValidateLabel() implemented with clear error messages
- Existing ValidateSessionName behavior unchanged
- Table-driven tests for all edge cases
- go vet and golangci-lint pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:42.356295869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:26.537826485Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:26.537810535Z","close_reason":"implemented: label primitives, validation, spawn flag, tests, GetProjectDir","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","label-feature"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:42.356295869Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:05.042485195Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cu02.4","title":"Add --label flag to ntm spawn command","description":"## Goal
Add the `--label` / `-l` flag to the `ntm spawn` command, which is the primary entry point for creating agent sessions.

## Context
`ntm spawn` is the main command users run to create multi-agent sessions. Currently: `ntm spawn myproject --cc=3`. With this change: `ntm spawn myproject --label frontend --cc=3` creates tmux session `myproject--frontend`, working in the SAME project directory as `myproject`.

Because GetProjectDir strips labels (task .13), the labeled session shares the base project directory. Multiple agent swarms can work on the same codebase with different goals.

## Implementation

### File: internal/cli/spawn.go

1. Add cobra flag in the spawn command setup:
```go
cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&opts.Label, "label", "l", "", "Goal label for multi-session support (creates session: project--label)")
```

2. In the RunE handler, early in the function (BEFORE session name is used anywhere):
```go
if opts.Label != "" {
    if err := config.ValidateLabel(opts.Label); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("invalid label: %w", err)
    }
    sessionName = config.FormatSessionName(sessionName, opts.Label)
}
```

3. The SpawnOptions struct needs a Label field:
```go
type SpawnOptions struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    Label string // Goal label for multi-session support
}
```

### Where exactly to insert the label logic
Look for where `sessionName` is first set from args[0]. The label transform must happen RIGHT AFTER arg parsing, BEFORE any call to GetProjectDir, CreateSession, or FormatPaneName. The label transforms the session name, then everything downstream uses the transformed name.

### What NOT to change in spawn.go
- GetProjectDir is already modified (task .13) to strip labels -- spawn just passes the labeled name
- CreateSession receives the full labeled name as the tmux session identifier -- correct
- FormatPaneName receives the full labeled name -- correct (pane names include session)

### User-facing behavior
```
$ ntm spawn myproject --label frontend --cc=2
Created session: myproject--frontend
  Project dir: ~/projects/myproject          <- SAME as base project
  Agents: 2x Claude Code

$ ntm spawn myproject --label backend --cc=3
Created session: myproject--backend
  Project dir: ~/projects/myproject          <- SAME dir, different session
  Agents: 3x Claude Code

$ ntm list
myproject--frontend  2 agents  running
myproject--backend   3 agents  running
```

Both sessions' agents work in ~/projects/myproject/. They coordinate via Agent Mail file reservations and see each other's code changes in real-time -- exactly like agents within a single session.

### Error cases
- `--label ""` -> error: label must not be empty
- `--label "my--label"` -> error: label must not contain '--'
- `--label "-bad"` -> error: label must start with alphanumeric
- `ntm spawn myproject--frontend` (no flag, but has --): works as before; GetProjectDir strips the label so it still maps to ~/projects/myproject/

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--label` / `-l` flag added to spawn command
- Label validated via ValidateLabel()
- Session name constructed via FormatSessionName()
- Project directory is the BASE project dir (label stripped by GetProjectDir)
- Existing spawns without --label are completely unchanged
- go build, go vet, golangci-lint pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:49.411284887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:34.638142061Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:34.638115341Z","close_reason":"implemented: --label flag on spawn command","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","label-feature"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:49.411284887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:00:40.585374570Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:05.400740197Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:05.751434851Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":304,"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"KEY IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: The label transform happens ONCE at the top of RunE, before the session name is used anywhere. This is the single point of change. After sessionName = FormatSessionName(base, label), every downstream call (GetProjectDir, CreateSession, FormatPaneName, Agent Mail registration, CASS context) receives the labeled name automatically. Do NOT sprinkle label-awareness throughout the function -- transform early, use everywhere.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:25:05Z"},{"id":309,"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTED: Previous description showed separate directories per label (myproject--frontend/ and myproject--backend/). This was wrong. With GetProjectDir stripping, both sessions share ~/projects/myproject/. Updated to match owner-approved design.","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:01:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3cu02.5","title":"Add --label flag to ntm create and ntm quick commands","description":"## Goal
Add the `--label` / `-l` flag to `ntm create` and `ntm quick` commands for consistency with spawn.

## Context
`ntm create` creates bare sessions (without spawning agents). `ntm quick` is a quick-spawn shortcut. Both accept a session name and should support the same `--label` flag as spawn.

Since GetProjectDir now strips labels (task .13), labeled sessions created via create/quick will also share the base project directory. The session name includes the label but the working directory does not.

## Implementation

### File: internal/cli/create.go
1. Add `--label` / `-l` flag to the create command
2. Same validation logic as spawn: ValidateLabel(), then FormatSessionName()
3. Insert label transformation right after session name is parsed from args, before any downstream use

### File: internal/cli/quick.go
1. Add `--label` / `-l` flag to the quick command
2. Same validation logic as spawn

### Pattern
The label handling in each command is identical:
```go
if labelFlag != "" {
    if err := config.ValidateLabel(labelFlag); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("invalid label: %w", err)
    }
    sessionName = config.FormatSessionName(sessionName, labelFlag)
}
```

This is intentionally NOT abstracted into a helper -- it is 5 lines of clear, explicit code. Premature abstraction would make it harder to understand and maintain. Each command owns its own flag registration and label handling.

### Verify -l flag availability
Check that `-l` is not already used by create or quick commands:
```
rg "\"l\"" internal/cli/create.go internal/cli/quick.go
```
If `-l` is taken, use a different short flag or omit the short form.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--label` / `-l` flag works on create and quick commands
- Label validation identical to spawn
- Labeled sessions share base project directory via GetProjectDir stripping
- Existing behavior without --label is unchanged
- go build, go vet, golangci-lint pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:56.788466393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:05:21.830741718Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","label-feature"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:21:56.788466393Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:00:40.943145817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:06.113199805Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:06.478482909Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3cu02.6","title":"Update ntm list to display label column","description":"## Goal
Update `ntm list` to detect and display labels when labeled sessions exist, with additional columns for project grouping.

## Context
`ntm list` shows all active sessions. With labeled sessions, users need to quickly see which sessions share a base project and what labels distinguish them. Since labeled sessions share a project directory, visual grouping by project name is important.

## Implementation

### File: internal/cli/list.go (or session.go -- wherever list rendering happens)

1. **Parse labels from session names**: Use `config.ParseSessionLabel(s.Name)` on each session
2. **Detect if any labels exist**: Scan all sessions; if any have labels, enable the extra columns
3. **Add PROJECT and LABEL columns**:

Without labels (unchanged):
```
SESSION       AGENTS  STATUS
myproject     3       running
otherproject  2       running
```

With labels present:
```
SESSION              PROJECT       LABEL      AGENTS  STATUS
myproject            myproject     -          2       running
myproject--frontend  myproject     frontend   3       running
myproject--backend   myproject     backend    2       running
otherproject         otherproject  -          1       running
```

Note: All three myproject sessions share the SAME project directory. The PROJECT column makes this relationship visible. Sessions with the same PROJECT value work on the same codebase.

4. **JSON output**: Always include `base_project` and `label` fields in JSON output (even without labels, for API consistency):
```json
{
  "name": "myproject--frontend",
  "base_project": "myproject",
  "label": "frontend",
  "project_dir": "/home/user/projects/myproject",
  ...
}
```

Note: `project_dir` is the SAME for all three myproject sessions. This is by design.

### Design decisions
- The extra columns only appear in text output when at least one session has a label. Avoids visual noise for users who never use labels.
- JSON output always includes the fields for API consistency.
- `-` is used for unlabeled sessions in the LABEL column for visual clarity.
- Sessions are sorted by base project name, then by label, for natural grouping.

### Sorting
Update the sort order to group related sessions:
```go
sort.Slice(sessions, func(i, j int) bool {
    bi, li := config.ParseSessionLabel(sessions[i].Name)
    bj, lj := config.ParseSessionLabel(sessions[j].Name)
    if bi != bj {
        return bi < bj
    }
    // Within same project: unlabeled first, then labels alphabetically
    if li == "" {
        return true
    }
    if lj == "" {
        return false
    }
    return li < lj
})
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm list shows PROJECT and LABEL columns when labeled sessions exist
- ntm list --json includes base_project and label fields always
- ntm list without labeled sessions is visually unchanged
- Sessions sorted by project, then unlabeled-first, then label alphabetically
- Parsing uses config.ParseSessionLabel (single source of truth)
- go build, go vet, golangci-lint pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:04.313439073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:03:37.900586089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","display","label-feature"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:04.313439073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:06.833016131Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.7","title":"Verify all session-targeting commands with labeled names","description":"## Goal
Verify that all session-targeting commands work correctly with labeled session names AND with multiple sessions sharing a project directory.

## Context
Two things to verify:
1. Commands handle `--` in session names correctly (no broken parsing)
2. Commands behave correctly when multiple sessions share a project directory

## Commands to Verify

### Session name with -- (should already work):
- `ntm attach myproject--frontend` -- passes name to tmux attach
- `ntm send myproject--frontend "msg"` -- passes name to tmux send-keys
- `ntm kill myproject--frontend` -- passes name to tmux kill-session
- `ntm add myproject--frontend` -- adds agent to existing session
- `ntm rotate myproject--frontend` -- rotates agents

### Shared directory concerns:
- `ntm status myproject--frontend` -- displays session info; should show label and shared dir
- `ntm checkpoint myproject--frontend` -- saves session state; verify checkpoint is keyed by full session name (not project dir)
- `ntm resume myproject--frontend` -- restores session; verify lookup uses session name

### Potential code patterns that break:

1. **strings.Split(session, "-")** -- would incorrectly split `myproject--frontend` into more pieces than expected. Search:
```
rg 'strings\.Split.*"-"' internal/cli/ --type go
```

2. **Regex patterns built from session name** without escaping. The `--` contains no regex metacharacters, but check anyway:
```
rg 'regexp.*session\|Compile.*session' internal/cli/ --type go
```

3. **Shell commands with unquoted session name**. Tmux send-keys with `--` could be interpreted as end-of-flags:
```
rg 'send-keys.*session\|tmux.*session' internal/cli/ internal/tmux/ --type go
```
IMPORTANT: Check how session names are passed to tmux commands. If using `tmux send-keys -t myproject--frontend`, the `--` might be interpreted as end of tmux flags. This would be a real bug. Solution: ensure session names are properly quoted or use `--` separator before the target:
```
tmux send-keys -t "myproject--frontend" ...
```

4. **Path construction outside GetProjectDir**. Any code that builds paths using session name directly (not via GetProjectDir) would create paths with `--` in them:
```
rg 'filepath\.Join.*session' internal/cli/ --type go
```

### Fix protocol
For each issue found:
1. Identify the specific line and the assumption being made
2. Fix it (use full session name as-is, or use SessionBase() where base name is needed)
3. Add a test case with a labeled session name
4. If the bug exists for regular dashed names too (like `my-project`), note this in the fix comment

## Acceptance Criteria
- All session-targeting commands verified with labeled names
- No command splits session names on single dash in a way that breaks --
- Tmux command invocations properly quote session names
- No path construction bypasses GetProjectDir using raw session name
- Fixes applied with tests for any issues found
- Manual verification: spawn labeled session, run each command against it","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:11.392495049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:06:14.881960986Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["label-feature","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:11.392495049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:06:14.881905331Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:07.190852559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":306,"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEARCH PATTERNS to find code that might break with -- in session names: (1) strings.Split(session, "-") would incorrectly split on every dash; (2) regexp using session name without escaping; (3) shell commands using session name without quoting. If any of these patterns exist, they are bugs even WITHOUT the label feature (session names like my-project already have dashes). The label feature just makes the bug more visible.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:25:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3cu02.8","title":"Handle inferSessionFromCWD disambiguation for shared project dirs","description":"## Goal
Add disambiguation logic to inferSessionFromCWD for when multiple labeled sessions share the same project directory.

## Context
`inferSessionFromCWD` (internal/cli/util.go:172-221) auto-detects the current session based on CWD. It finds the session whose project directory is the longest prefix match.

### The Problem (now real)
With GetProjectDir stripping labels:
- `GetProjectDir("myproject")` -> `~/projects/myproject/`
- `GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend")` -> `~/projects/myproject/`
- `GetProjectDir("myproject--backend")` -> `~/projects/myproject/`

If the user is in `~/projects/myproject/` and all three sessions are running, the longest-prefix-match produces THREE equal-length matches. The current code picks the first one found, which is non-deterministic.

### Solution: Three-tier disambiguation

Modify inferSessionFromCWD to handle ties:

```go
// After collecting all matches with equal-length projectDir:
if len(matches) == 1 {
    return matches[0].Name, "current directory" // unambiguous
}

if len(matches) > 1 {
    // Tier 1: prefer the unlabeled (base) session if it exists
    for _, m := range matches {
        if !config.HasLabel(m.Name) {
            return m.Name, "current directory (base session preferred)"
        }
    }

    // Tier 2: if all are labeled, return error with helpful message
    names := make([]string, len(matches))
    for i, m := range matches {
        names[i] = m.Name
    }
    return "", fmt.Sprintf(
        "multiple sessions share this project directory: %s. "+
        "Specify the session name explicitly.",
        strings.Join(names, ", "),
    )
}
```

### Why this three-tier approach

1. **Single match**: No change from current behavior.
2. **Prefer unlabeled**: If you have `myproject` AND `myproject--frontend`, and you're in the project dir, you probably mean the "main" session. Labeled sessions are the specialized ones -- if you want them, specify the name.
3. **All labeled, no base**: Ambiguous. The user must specify. This is safe and explicit.

### Edge cases to test
- Only unlabeled session: returns it (unchanged from today)
- Only one labeled session: returns it (no ambiguity)
- Unlabeled + one labeled: returns unlabeled (Tier 1)
- Unlabeled + multiple labeled: returns unlabeled (Tier 1)
- Two labeled, no unlabeled: returns error listing both (Tier 2)
- User in subdirectory of project: same logic, prefix match still works
- User in unrelated directory: no match, unchanged behavior

### Implementation details

File: `internal/cli/util.go`

The current code (lines 191-205) collects matches:
```go
for _, s := range sessions {
    projectDir := filepath.Clean(activeCfg.GetProjectDir(s.Name))
    if cwd == projectDir || strings.HasPrefix(cwd, projectDir+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
        if len(projectDir) > bestLen {
            bestName = s.Name
            bestLen = len(projectDir)
        }
    }
}
```

Change to collect ALL matches at the best length, then disambiguate:
```go
var bestLen int
var bestMatches []SessionInfo
for _, s := range sessions {
    projectDir := filepath.Clean(activeCfg.GetProjectDir(s.Name))
    if cwd == projectDir || strings.HasPrefix(cwd, projectDir+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
        dirLen := len(projectDir)
        if dirLen > bestLen {
            bestMatches = []SessionInfo{s}
            bestLen = dirLen
        } else if dirLen == bestLen {
            bestMatches = append(bestMatches, s)
        }
    }
}
// Then apply three-tier disambiguation on bestMatches
```

### Basename fallback (lines 207-220)
The existing basename fallback also needs checking:
```go
base := filepath.Base(cwd)
for _, s := range sessions {
    if s.Name == base {
        matches++
        matchName = s.Name
    }
}
```

This compares the directory basename to session names. Since labeled sessions share a dir, `filepath.Base("~/projects/myproject")` is `myproject`, which matches the unlabeled session name but NOT `myproject--frontend`. This is correct -- the basename fallback naturally prefers the unlabeled session.

## Acceptance Criteria
- inferSessionFromCWD correctly disambiguates when multiple sessions share a project dir
- Unlabeled session preferred when available (Tier 1)
- Clear error message when ambiguous with only labeled sessions (Tier 2)
- All edge cases tested with table-driven unit tests
- Existing behavior unchanged for unlabeled-only scenarios
- go test passes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:18.535157620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:02:02.824749931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["label-feature","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:18.535157620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:00:41.301117169Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":305,"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPORTANT REALIZATION: Since our design gives each labeled session its own directory (myproject--frontend/ is DIFFERENT from myproject--backend/), inferSessionFromCWD has NO ambiguity problem. The original GH issue raised this as a critical issue, but that was based on the assumption that GetProjectDir would strip labels and return the same base dir. Our design avoids this entirely. This task is primarily verification that the existing code works, not a code change.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:25:19Z"},{"id":310,"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTED: Previous description said 'NO ambiguity problem' and 'just tests confirming it works.' This was wrong. With GetProjectDir stripping labels, multiple sessions DO share the same directory, creating REAL ambiguity. Rewrote task to implement three-tier disambiguation logic (prefer unlabeled > error with options). This is now a CODE CHANGE task, not just a verification task.","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:02:02Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.9","title":"Comprehensive unit tests for label feature","description":"## Goal
Comprehensive unit tests for all label-related functionality including parsing, validation, GetProjectDir label stripping, and inferSessionFromCWD disambiguation.

## Test Files

### internal/config/label_test.go
Table-driven tests for all label primitives:

```go
func TestParseSessionLabel(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct{
        input, wantBase, wantLabel string
    }{
        {"myproject", "myproject", ""},
        {"myproject--frontend", "myproject", "frontend"},
        {"my-project--frontend", "my-project", "frontend"},
        {"foo--bar--baz", "foo", "bar--baz"},
        {"proj--my-label", "proj", "my-label"},
        {"--frontend", "", "frontend"},          // degenerate: empty base
        {"myproject--", "myproject", ""},         // degenerate: empty label
        {"a--b", "a", "b"},                      // minimal
        {"abc", "abc", ""},                      // no separator
        {"a-b-c--d-e-f", "a-b-c", "d-e-f"},    // dashes everywhere
    }
}

func TestFormatSessionName(t *testing.T) {
    // Round-trip: FormatSessionName(ParseSessionLabel(x)) == x for valid inputs
    // FormatSessionName("base", "") == "base"
    // FormatSessionName("base", "label") == "base--label"
}

func TestHasLabel(t *testing.T) { /* true for "a--b", false for "a-b" */ }
func TestSessionBase(t *testing.T) { /* wrapper correctness */ }

func TestValidateLabel(t *testing.T) {
    valid := []string{"frontend", "backend", "bugfix-123", "my_label", "a", "A1b2C3"}
    invalid := []struct{ label, errContains string }{
        {"", "empty"},
        {strings.Repeat("a", 51), "50 characters"},
        {"my--label", "separator"},
        {"-bad", "alphanumeric"},
        {"_bad", "alphanumeric"},
        {"bad!", "alphanumeric"},
        {"bad label", "alphanumeric"},
    }
}
```

### internal/config/config_test.go (add to existing)
Verify GetProjectDir with label stripping:

```go
func TestGetProjectDir_WithLabel(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &Config{ProjectsBase: "/home/user/projects"}

    // Unlabeled: unchanged behavior
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/myproject", cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject"))

    // Labeled: label stripped, returns base project dir
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/myproject", cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend"))
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/myproject", cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--backend"))

    // Multiple sessions resolve to SAME directory
    dir1 := cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject")
    dir2 := cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend")
    dir3 := cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--backend")
    assert.Equal(t, dir1, dir2)
    assert.Equal(t, dir2, dir3)

    // Different projects still resolve to different dirs
    assert.NotEqual(t, cfg.GetProjectDir("project-a"), cfg.GetProjectDir("project-b"))

    // Dashes in project name preserved
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/my-project", cfg.GetProjectDir("my-project--label"))
}
```

### internal/cli/util_test.go (add to existing)
Test inferSessionFromCWD disambiguation:

```go
func TestInferSessionFromCWD_LabelDisambiguation(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct{
        name     string
        sessions []string  // active session names
        cwd      string
        wantName string
        wantErr  bool
    }{
        {
            name: "single unlabeled",
            sessions: []string{"myproject"},
            cwd: "/home/user/projects/myproject",
            wantName: "myproject",
        },
        {
            name: "single labeled",
            sessions: []string{"myproject--frontend"},
            cwd: "/home/user/projects/myproject",
            wantName: "myproject--frontend",
        },
        {
            name: "unlabeled preferred over labeled",
            sessions: []string{"myproject", "myproject--frontend"},
            cwd: "/home/user/projects/myproject",
            wantName: "myproject", // Tier 1: prefer unlabeled
        },
        {
            name: "unlabeled preferred over multiple labeled",
            sessions: []string{"myproject", "myproject--fe", "myproject--be"},
            cwd: "/home/user/projects/myproject",
            wantName: "myproject",
        },
        {
            name: "multiple labeled only - ambiguous",
            sessions: []string{"myproject--frontend", "myproject--backend"},
            cwd: "/home/user/projects/myproject",
            wantErr: true, // Tier 2: error with options
        },
        {
            name: "different projects not confused",
            sessions: []string{"alpha--fe", "beta--fe"},
            cwd: "/home/user/projects/alpha",
            wantName: "alpha--fe",
        },
    }
}
```

### internal/cli/spawn_test.go (add to existing)
Test spawn label integration:
- Spawn with --label: verify sessionName == "base--label"
- Spawn without --label: verify sessionName == "base" (unchanged)
- Spawn with invalid --label: verify error returned
- Spawn with --label: verify GetProjectDir returns base dir

## Acceptance Criteria
- 100% line coverage for label.go
- GetProjectDir label-stripping tested with all variants
- inferSessionFromCWD disambiguation tested with all tiers
- All edge cases covered in table-driven tests
- go test ./internal/config/ ./internal/cli/ pass
- No test flakiness","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:25.403362262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:10.443032198Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["label-feature","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:22:25.403362262Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:10.088388636Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:07.895234451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:08.246160388Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:08.597923613Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:08.955266948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:23:09.316114655Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:10.442951036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cw6i","title":"EPIC: Command Kernel + Parity Gate","description":"## Summary\nBuild the Command Kernel registry to enforce mechanical parity between CLI, TUI, REST, OpenAPI, and Web UI command palette.\n\n## Scope\n- Create internal/kernel registry with command metadata, schemas, and REST bindings.\n- Refactor internal/robot into GetX() + PrintX() to share structs with API.\n- Extract service layer (SessionService, AgentService, OutputService, etc.).\n- Wire CLI/TUI commands through kernel (no ad‑hoc cobra actions).\n- Parity Gate CI: kernel coverage + OpenAPI spec diff + CLI vs API output parity.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Every CLI command is registered in kernel with REST + OpenAPI metadata.\n- OpenAPI generated from kernel matches checked‑in spec.\n- Parity tests prevent drift.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:59.274688688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:47.535499508Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-1nqde","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:47.535457749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-1wsxq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:37.151170552Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-1xc00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:24.811218097Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:42.414186876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:32.118200628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Handle corrupted state file
- Handle missing state file
- Concurrent read/write safety

### State Transitions
- assigned -> working -> completed
- assigned -> failed
- Clear assignment on pane kill
- Handle duplicate assignments

## Logging Requirements
Each test should log state before/after operations with timestamps.
